user.title: Tariff configuration — pick utility, date & power
user.description:
Pick the utility, the maximum output (kWp) of the PV system and the date when the rate configuration takes effect.

References:
- [Federal floor table (EnV Art. 12 Abs. 1bis)]({env_url})
- [BFE Einspeisevergütung overview]({agency_url})

tariff_pick.title: Tariff configuration ({utility_name}) — pick date & power
tariff_pick.description: Pick the date this configuration takes effect and your plant's installed power. The next step shows utility-specific options (self-consumption, HKN, utility-specific fields).

data_description.valid_from:
## Plant install date
The date your PV plant came online (or this configuration started applying). The integration uses this as the anchor of your config history — quarters before this date are silently skipped during recompute since no tariff configuration covers them.

Default: current quarter start. Set to your actual install date for a clean import history.
data_description.installierte_leistung_kwp:
## Installed peak power
The DC peak rating of your PV plant in kilowatts. Used to look up the federal **Mindestvergütung** floor ([EnV Art. 12 Abs. 1bis]({env_url}), AS 2025 138, in force since 1.1.2026):

| Size | Mode | Federal floor (Rp/kWh) |
|---|---|---|
| <30 kW | any | **6.00** |
| 30–<150 kW | with self-consumption | **180÷kW** (1.20–6.00) |
| 30–<150 kW | feed-in only | **6.20** |
| ≥150 kW | any | no federal floor |

tariff_details.title: Tariff configuration — utility-specific options

data.aew_fixpreis_rmp: Compensation model (fixed price / reference market price)
data.fixpreis_rmp: Compensation model (fixed price / reference market price)

data_description.eigenverbrauch_aktiviert:
## Self-consumption
**Yes** if any portion of your PV production is consumed on-site (typical residential / commercial setup). **No** for full feed-in plants (Volleinspeisung) where 100% of production goes to the grid.

This flag affects the federal floor band you fall into — for 30–<150 kW plants the difference is significant (degressive 180÷kW with self-consumption vs. flat 6.20 Rp/kWh without).
data_description.hkn_aktiviert: You'll get more for your feed-in solar power if you sell the **guarantee of origin (GO)** to your utility.

entities.title: Entity wiring
entities.description: Link the kWh counter for your grid export, the CHF sensor that the Energy Dashboard auto-creates, and a plant name.

data_description.stromnetzeinspeisung_kwh:
## kWh counter
The **kWh counter** that tracks energy fed into the grid.

Must already be wired into the **Energy Dashboard** under *'Return to grid'*.
data_description.rueckliefervergutung_chf:
## Auto-created CHF sensor
The **CHF sensor** that the Energy Dashboard auto-creates when you configure *'Return to grid with a price'*.

HA names it with a `_compensation` suffix (usually `sensor.YOUR_GRID_EXPORT_compensation`). This integration writes into its **Long-Term Statistics**.
data_description.plant_name: Display name for this PV plant - e.g. `PV Plant`. Used as the integration's title in Settings → Devices & Services & as the default for the entity name prefix below.
data_description.namenspraefix:
Prefix for this integration's *sensors* (e.g. `sensor.PREFIX_basisverguetung`).

Leave empty to auto-derive from the plant name (e.g. `sensor.pv_plant_basisverguetung`).

init.title: BFE Rückliefertarif options
init.description: What would you like to do?

manage_history.title: Manage configuration history
manage_history.description: Click a row to edit, choose **Add new transition** to record a switch (e.g. utility change, PV expansion, HKN certificate), or **Done** to finish. Past quarter recompute uses the values that were active back then.

recompute_history.title: Recompute full history
recompute_history.description:
Rewrites all Long-Term Statistics for every BFE-published quarter using your current configuration history (per-quarter utility / HKN / kW). Also writes a conservative **estimate** for the in-progress quarter using the active utility's effective floor (HKN, fixed price, or federal minimum).

Idempotent and safe to re-run.

refresh_data.title: Refresh prices & tariff data
refresh_data.description:
Force a fresh poll of the BFE reference market prices AND a fresh fetch of the companion repo's tariff database (skips the 6-hour scheduled tick and the daily tariff cache). Auto-imports any quarter BFE has published since the last successful poll.

Use this whenever you've pushed a fix to the `bfe-tariffs-data` repo and want HA to pick it up immediately.

## kWh counter
The **kWh counter** that tracks energy fed into the grid.

Must already be wired into the **Energy Dashboard** under *'Return to grid'*.
data_description.rueckliefervergutung_chf:
## Auto-created CHF sensor
The **CHF sensor** that the Energy Dashboard auto-creates when you configure *'Return to grid with a price'*.

HA names it with a `_compensation` suffix (usually `sensor.YOUR_GRID_EXPORT_compensation`). This integration writes into its **Long-Term Statistics**.
data_description.plant_name: Display name for this PV plant - e.g. `PV Plant`. Used as the integration's title in Settings → Devices & Services.
data_description.namenspraefix:
Prefix for this integration's *sensors* (e.g. `sensor.PREFIX_basisverguetung`).

⚠️ Changing this orphans **Long-Term Statistics** tied to the old prefix — only change if you understand the consequence!

init.title: Reconfigure {utility} from {period_from}
init.description: Your saved configuration covers {period_from} → {period_to}. The tariff structure has changed and these fields need a value: **{fields}**. Pick now — saving appends a new history entry at {period_from} with your choices.
